title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Layered Failure Evidence
summary: A fleet-control CLI calls an inventory service through a cache and gateway, but operators cannot tell whether failures come from naming, transport, status, or decoding. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go layered failure evidence increment in a Go 1.26 net/http protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify name resolution, connection, TLS, protocol, status, representation, and business failures without collapsing them. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Explain protocol statelessness while locating cookies, tokens, caches, and workflow state outside core request semantics. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: State method semantics, target authority, credential scope, body ownership, byte and time budgets, and expected status or schema; trace resolution, transport, redirects, and attempts; then test one changed failure with deterministic cleanup evidence. Apply that move specifically to Layered Failure Evidence while producing a Go 1.26 net/http protocol evidence map.
